title: Morphological and Molecular Identification of Porang Plants in Manggarai Regency, East Nusa Tenggara
author: Ignasius Edward Ileng
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Porang (Amorphophallus sp.) is a high-value tropical commodity due to its glucomannan content, which is widely utilized in the food and pharmaceutical industries. Accurate species identification is essential to ensure genetic purity and support sustainable cultivation. This study combines morphological and molecular approaches to identify Porang plants from Desa Sita, East Manggarai Regency. Morphological characteristics were observed on the leaves, pseudostems, and corms. Molecular analysis was conducted through DNA amplification using the chloroplast matK gene marker, followed by DNA sequencing, BLAST analysis, and phylogenetic reconstruction using the Maximum Likelihood method with 1000 bootstrap replicates. Morphological observations revealed typical features of the genus Amorphophallus, including compound leaves, cylindrical pseudostems, and corm-type tubers. PCR amplification produced a single DNA band of approximately 800 bp, indicating successful target gene amplification. Homology analysis showed 90–100% sequence similarity with reference species. Phylogenetic analysis demonstrated that Porang 1 is closely related to Amorphophallus bulbifer, while Porang 2 clusters with to Amorphophallus muelleri. The integration of morphological identification and matK DNA barcoding proves effective in confirming taxonomic identity and genetic relationships of Porang plants. This approach has potential to support germplasm conservation and the development of Porang as a sustainable bioindustrial commodity in Indonesia.

keywords: porang, DNA barcode, matK, phylogenetics, molecular identification
